title: agrestis canina
intro: Agrestis canina, also known as Dog's Mercury, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Erophoraceae. Traditionally, Dog's Mercury has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a purgative and emetic, helping to relieve digestive issues and induce sweating to reduce fever. The plant's health benefits extend to its potential use in treating conditions such as arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, although its use is not recommended due to its toxicity. In herbal preparations, Dog's Mercury is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves, but its preparation and administration should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
